title: Complete Beginner
description: Start from zero and learn the absolute basics of Dutch

units.title: Greetings & Introductions
units.description: Learn how to greet people, say goodbye, and introduce yourself in Dutch

lessons.title: First Hellos
lessons.description: Learn the essential Dutch greetings for different times of day

exercises.question: Which greeting do you use in the morning?

lessons.title: Saying Goodbye
lessons.description: Master the Dutch farewells for every situation

lessons.title: How Are You?
lessons.description: Ask and answer how someone is doing in Dutch

exercises.sentence: — Hoe gaat het? — ___, dank je wel.

lessons.title: My Name Is...
lessons.description: Introduce yourself and learn how to ask others their names

exercises.question: How do you say 'My name is Emma'?

lessons.title: Where Are You From?
lessons.description: Talk about where you come from and your nationality

exercises.question: How do you say 'I come from...' in Dutch?
What does 'Ik kom uit Nederland' mean?
options: I am going to the Netherlands

lessons.title: Unit 1 Review
lessons.description: Review everything from Unit 1 — greetings, farewells, and introductions

exercises.question: How do you say 'good morning' in Dutch?

units.title: Numbers 1–20
units.description: Master Dutch numbers from one to twenty — essential for shopping, addresses, and everyday life

lessons.title: One to Five
lessons.description: Learn the Dutch numbers one through five

lessons.description: Learn the Dutch numbers six through ten

lessons.description: Learn the Dutch numbers eleven through fifteen

lessons.title: Sixteen to Twenty
lessons.description: Learn the Dutch numbers sixteen through twenty

exercises.question: How do you say 'twenty' in Dutch?

exercises.sentence: Er zijn ___ leerlingen in de klas.
exercises.translation: There are ___ students in the class.

lessons.title: Counting in Context
lessons.description: Use numbers in real sentences about quantities

exercises.question: How do you say 'I have three dogs'?

lessons.title: Unit 2 Review
lessons.description: Review all Dutch numbers from 1 to 20 and using them in context

exercises.sentence: Er zijn ___ maanden in een jaar.
exercises.translation: There are ___ months in a year.

exercises.question: How do you say 'there are nine chairs'?

units.title: Essential Everyday Words
units.description: The most useful words for navigating everyday situations in the Netherlands

lessons.title: Yes, No & Maybe
lessons.description: Learn how to agree, disagree, and express uncertainty in Dutch

lessons.title: Please & Thank You
lessons.description: Master essential polite phrases in Dutch

exercises.question: How do you say 'you're welcome' in Dutch?

exercises.correctAnswer: Dank je wel voor de hulp.

lessons.title: Basic Question Words
lessons.description: Learn essential Dutch question words

lessons.title: Common Adjectives
lessons.description: Learn essential Dutch adjectives to describe the world around you

lessons.title: Time & Place Words
lessons.description: Learn Dutch words for time and location

exercises.sentence: De supermarkt is ___, bij de hoek.
exercises.translation: The supermarket is ___, at the corner.
Go straight ahead and then left.

lessons.title: Unit 3 Review
lessons.description: Review all essential everyday words from Unit 3

exercises.correctAnswer: Het huis is mooi en groot.
exercises.translation: The house is beautiful and big.

units.title: Simple Sentences
units.description: Build your first Dutch sentences using core verbs: zijn, hebben, willen

lessons.title: I Am...
lessons.description: Learn the verb 'zijn' (to be) and describe yourself and others

exercises.question: How do you say 'she is' in Dutch?
What is the correct form: 'Wij ___ vrienden'?

lessons.title: This Is / That Is
lessons.description: Learn to point out and identify things in Dutch

exercises.question: How do you ask 'what is this?' in Dutch?

lessons.title: I Have...
lessons.description: Learn the verb 'hebben' (to have) and talk about possessions

exercises.question: What is the correct form: 'Hij ___ twee katten'?
How do you say 'I have a bike' in Dutch?

lessons.title: I Want / I Would Like
lessons.description: Express wants and wishes politely in Dutch

exercises.question: What is the polite way to say 'I want a coffee'?
options: Ik wil graag een koffie. and Mag ik een koffie? are both correct
exercises.correctAnswer: Ik wil graag een koffie. and Mag ik een koffie? are both correct

lessons.title: Where Is...?
lessons.description: Ask for and give simple directions in Dutch

exercises.question: How do you say 'where is the supermarket?' in Dutch?

exercises.dutch: Het is daar, aan de linkerkant.
exercises.question: What did you hear?
options: Het is hier, aan de rechterkant.
Het is daar, aan de linkerkant.
exercises.correctAnswer: Het is daar, aan de linkerkant.

lessons.title: Unit 4 Review
lessons.description: Review all simple sentences from Unit 4 — zijn, hebben, willen, and directions

exercises.english: I would like a tea, please.
exercises.correctAnswer: Ik wil graag een thee, alsjeblieft.

exercises.question: What did you hear?
options: Hoe kom ik bij het station?

exercises.correctAnswer: May I have a coffee, please?

units.title: Basic Questions
units.description: Learn how to ask simple questions in Dutch

lessons.title: Question Words
lessons.description: Learn the essential Dutch question words

lessons.title: Where Questions
lessons.description: Asking and answering where questions

exercises.question: How do you ask 'Where do you live?' in Dutch?
What does 'Waar is de supermarkt?' mean?

lessons.title: Who & What
lessons.description: Asking who and what questions

exercises.question: How do you ask 'What is this?' in Dutch?
What does 'Wie is jouw leraar?' mean?

lessons.title: When & How Many
lessons.description: Asking about time and quantity

exercises.question: What does 'Hoe laat is het?' mean?

exercises.translation: How many apples do you want?

lessons.title: Yes/No Questions
lessons.description: Forming and answering yes/no questions

exercises.question: How do you turn 'Jij spreekt Nederlands' into a yes/no question?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 5 content

exercises.translation: What is your name? (lit. How are you called?)

exercises.question: How do you say 'Are you a student?' in Dutch?

exercises.translation: Do you want coffee or tea?

units.title: The Alphabet & Pronunciation
units.description: Master the Dutch alphabet and key pronunciation rules

lessons.title: The Dutch Alphabet
lessons.description: Learn all 26 letters of the Dutch alphabet

exercises.question: How many letters does the Dutch alphabet have?
The letter 'g' in Dutch is pronounced like which sound?
options: a guttural sound from the throat
exercises.correctAnswer: a guttural sound from the throat

exercises.translation: The Dutch alphabet has twenty-six letters.

exercises.correctAnswer: De letter 'g' klinkt anders dan in het Engels.
exercises.translation: The letter 'g' sounds different than in English.

lessons.title: Vowel Sounds
lessons.description: Dutch short and long vowel pronunciation

exercises.question: A Dutch 'long a' (aa) sounds most like which English word?
Which word has a long vowel sound?

exercises.sentence: Het woord 'raam' heeft een lange ___ a.
exercises.translation: The word 'raam' has a long vowel a.

exercises.correctAnswer: Dutch heeft korte en lange klinkers.
exercises.translation: Dutch has short and long vowels.

lessons.title: Consonants & Special Sounds
lessons.description: Dutch consonant sounds that differ from English

exercises.question: The Dutch 'r' is typically pronounced how?
The Dutch 'w' sounds closest to which English sound?

exercises.sentence: De 'g' in het Nederlands is een ___ klank.
exercises.translation: The 'g' in Dutch is a guttural sound.

exercises.correctAnswer: De uitspraak van de 'g' is moeilijk.
exercises.translation: The pronunciation of the 'g' is difficult.

exercises.question: At the end of a word, 'b' and 'd' in Dutch are often pronounced as which sounds?

lessons.title: Dutch Digraphs
lessons.description: Learn the special two-letter combinations: ij/ei, ou/au, ui, oe, eu

exercises.question: The Dutch 'ij' sound is closest to which English sound?
'ij' and 'ei' in Dutch sound...
options: one is long, one is short

exercises.correctAnswer: De klank 'ui' bestaat niet in het Engels.
exercises.translation: The sound 'ui' does not exist in English.

lessons.title: Stress & Rhythm
lessons.description: Learn where to place stress in Dutch words

exercises.question: In most Dutch words, where does the stress fall?

exercises.correctAnswer: De klemtoon ligt op de eerste lettergreep.
exercises.translation: The stress falls on the first syllable.

lessons.title: Spelling & Spelling Out
lessons.description: Learn to spell words aloud in Dutch

exercises.question: How do you say 'Can you spell that?' in Dutch?
